heading: Treatment of certain qualified productions
label: 181 Treatment of certain qualified productions

children.heading: Election to treat costs as expenses
children.label: a Election to treat costs as expenses

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A taxpayer may elect to treat the cost of any qualified film or television production, any qualified live theatrical production, and any qualified sound recording production as an expense which is not chargeable to capital account. Any cost so treated shall be allowed as a deduction.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to so much of the aggregate cost of any qualified film or television production or any qualified live theatrical production as exceeds $15,000,000.

children.heading: Higher dollar limitation for productions in certain areas
children.label: B Higher dollar limitation for productions in certain areas
i
children.text: a low-income community under section 45D, or

children.label: ii
children.text: a distressed county or isolated area of distress by the Delta Regional Authority established under , United States Code,section 2009aa–1 of title 7

children.heading: Qualified sound recording production
children.label: C Qualified sound recording production
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to so much of the aggregate cost of any qualified sound recording production, or to so much of the aggregate, cumulative cost of all such qualified sound recording productions in the taxable year, as exceeds $150,000.

children.heading: No other deduction or amortization deduction allowable
children.label: b No other deduction or amortization deduction allowable
children.text: With respect to the basis of any qualified film or television production, any qualified live theatrical production, or any qualified sound recording production to which an election is made under subsection (a), no other depreciation or amortization deduction shall be allowable.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An election under this section with respect to any qualified film or television production, any qualified live theatrical production, or any qualified sound recording production shall be made in such manner as prescribed by the Secretary and by the due date (including extensions) for filing the taxpayer’s return of tax under this chapter for the taxable year in which costs of the production are first incurred.

children.heading: Revocation of election
children.label: 2 Revocation of election
children.text: Any election made under this section may not be revoked without the consent of the Secretary.

children.heading: Qualified film or television production
children.label: d Qualified film or television production

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “qualified film or television production” means any production described in paragraph (2) if 75 percent of the total compensation of the production is qualified compensation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A production is described in this paragraph if such production is property described in section 168(f)(3).

children.heading: Special rules for television series
children.label: B Special rules for television series
i
children.text: each episode of such series shall be treated as a separate production, and

children.label: ii
children.text: only the first 44 episodes of such series shall be taken into account.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: C Exception
children.text: A production is not described in this paragraph if records are required under , United States Code, to be maintained with respect to any performer in such production.section 2257 of title 18

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “qualified compensation” means compensation for services performed in the United States by actors, production personnel, directors, and producers.

children.heading: Participations and residuals excluded
children.label: B Participations and residuals excluded
children.text: The term “compensation” does not include participations and residuals (as defined in section 167(g)(7)(B)).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “qualified live theatrical production” means any production described in paragraph (2) if 75 percent of the total compensation of the production is qualified compensation (as defined in subsection (d)(3)).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A production is described in this paragraph if such production is a live staged production of a play (with or without music) which is derived from a written book or script and is produced or presented by a taxable entity in any venue which has an audience capacity of not more than 3,000 or a series of venues the majority of which have an audience capacity of not more than 3,000.

children.label: i
children.text: for which the election under this section would be allowable to the same taxpayer, and

children.label: I
children.text: separate phases of a production, or

children.label: II
children.text: separate simultaneous stagings of the same production in different geographical locations (not including multiple performance locations of any one touring production),

children.label: i
children.text: The initial staging of a live theatrical production.

children.label: ii
children.text: Subsequent additional stagings or touring of such production which are produced by the same producer as the initial staging.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of a live staged production not described in subparagraph (B) which is produced or presented by a taxable entity for not more than 10 weeks of the taxable year, subparagraph (A) shall be applied by substituting “6,500” for “3,000”.

children.heading: Short taxable years
children.label: ii Short taxable years
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), in the case of any taxable year of less than 12 months, the number of weeks for which a production is produced or presented shall be annualized by multiplying the number of weeks the production is produced or presented during such taxable year by 12 and dividing the result by the number of months in such taxable year.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: E Exception
children.text: A production is not described in this paragraph if such production includes or consists of any performance of conduct described in , United States Code.section 2257(h)(1) of title 18

children.heading: Qualified sound recording production
children.label: f Qualified sound recording production
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “qualified sound recording production” means a sound recording (as defined in , United States Code) produced and recorded in the United States.section 101 of title 17

children.heading: Application of certain other rules
children.label: g Application of certain other rules
children.text: For purposes of this section, rules similar to the rules of subsections (b)(2) and (c)(4) of section 194 shall apply.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: h Termination
children.text: This section shall not apply to qualified film and television productions, qualified live theatrical productions, or qualified sound recording productions commencing after .December 31, 2025
